briefing.opening: Good morning. Welcome to the Climate Briefing for April 13, 2026. Today, we will cover various efforts to address climate change both domestically and internationally, alongside reports on the deepening phenomena of the climate crisis.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Climate Action and Policy Reinforcement Efforts
sections.content: Domestically, efforts to raise awareness of the climate crisis and promote carbon neutrality practices are active through the operation of Climate Change Week. Wonju City and Gyeyang District will operate Climate Change Week from April 20 to 24, encouraging carbon neutrality practices to commemorate the 56th Earth Day [1, 3, 7, 11, 12]. Furthermore, Seongnam City is engaging citizens through education and campaigns, such as providing carbon neutrality and climate crisis response training to 240 environmental professionals [22, 26, 27, 29]. In the National Assembly, a 'policy recommendations document' was submitted to strengthen climate response implementation, and amendments to the Carbon Neutrality Framework Act are scheduled for discussion this month [5, 18]. During the public deliberation process for the Carbon Neutrality Act amendment, a high public demand for early greenhouse gas reduction was confirmed, with 78% of citizens supporting it [15, 25].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Current Challenges
sections.content: Amidst the energy crisis originating from the Middle East, there is a call to explicitly designate renewable energy as a 'strategic asset,' drawing attention to the acceleration of domestic renewable energy transition [31, 35]. Gyeongsangbuk-do is promoting support projects for fostering climate tech companies, and KEPCO KPS and Jeonnam Development Corporation are jointly responding to energy transition through renewable energy cooperation, continuing efforts to expand renewable energy infrastructure [9, 34]. In particular, the mandatory solar power project for public parking lots is being actively pursued, centered around Korea Midland Power and Boryeong City [37, 41, 42]. NH Nonghyup Bank is investing 207 billion won in the Shinan Uido offshore wind power project, contributing to renewable energy expansion and regional economic support [39, 44]. Meanwhile, the Climate Ministry is seeking to improve the regulatory system by promoting the establishment of an 'Electricity Regulatory Agency' to expand renewable energy [45], but reports of serious overcapacity issues in China's renewable energy sector highlight the importance of international balance and cooperation [38].

sections.title: ⚠️ Deepening Global Climate Change Impacts
sections.content: Warnings continue regarding the deepening direct impacts of climate change worldwide. Island nations like Vanuatu are facing challenges in the era of the climate crisis [13], and in New Jersey and Arizona, USA, climate change is recognized as a reality, with growing concerns about potential fatalities [49, 50]. Ski industry experts are expressing concerns about the impact of climate change on their activities [52, 54], and changes are also being detected in daily life, with student-athletes experiencing heat [53]. The steady decline in Atlantic ocean currents warns of potential amplification of global warming and disruption of the climate system [70]. Attention is required for extreme weather phenomena, including an increase in tropical storm frequency, a surge in climate disasters, and the potential development of an unusually strong El Niño phenomenon this summer [69, 80].

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Response Efforts and Research
sections.content: The international community continues various efforts and research to address climate change. The United States is making progress in responding to climate change [67], and the climate change consulting market is expected to grow by $3.34 billion from 2026 to 2030, indicating anticipated development in related industries [63]. IFT is exploring practical solutions by sharing climate change mitigation and adaptation strategies to improve food and nutrition security in a new white paper [60, 74]. In Latin America, water is being treated as a macroeconomic priority, with a focus on climate change investment and strengthening resilience [75]. Meanwhile, scientific efforts to understand and adapt to the complex mechanisms of climate change are also active, including research on how microparticles in Arctic ponds are linked to cloud formation and climate change [55], and news that McGill University's new greenhouse will help cultivate agriculture more resilient to climate change [76].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has clearly highlighted both the proactive domestic and international movements to address climate change, as well as the intensifying phenomena of the climate crisis. We must continue to show interest and cooperate to overcome the climate crisis and build a sustainable future. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: Experts say climate pattern could supercharge extreme weather events and push temperatures to record highsThere is a high likelihood that the phenomenon known as “El Niño” will emerge this summer – and it could be exceptionally strong. summary.phenomenon: There is a high likelihood that the 'El Niño' phenomenon will emerge this summer, potentially with exceptional strength. Meteorologists are closely monitoring climate patterns developing in the Pacific Ocean.
summary.outlook: Experts predict that a 'super El Niño,' if it develops, could intensify extreme weather events and push global temperatures to record highs next year.
